rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CONNIE M. MARATAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/25/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) Now that the conservatee is deceased, who is or are her successor(s)-in-interest? (2) A second supplement to the (second) amended first account must be filed to identify them and set forth their addresses. (3) Has an estate been opened yet for her? (4) Why haven't previously ordered fees and costs been paid? They were not on Schedule G (liabilities). (The conservator was already ordered on 2/28/24 to file her final accounting by no later than 5/29/24. The 6/10/24 status hearing set for the filing of that accounting was continued to this date (9/23/24) and will be continued again until this first account is adjudicated.) Deadline for providing the assigned examiner with courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings is 11/15/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MACPRIN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Independent Successor Trustee To Administer And Terminate The Macprin Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/18/2024, as the following issues (initially e-mailed to counsel on 06/26/2024 and subsequently posted as tentative rulings) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) The name and address of Terry Thompson's personal representative must be provided by a verified allegation, and notice must be given. Please also provide a copy of letters issued to the personal representative. If there is no personal representative, each heir of Terry Thompson and each devisee and executor named in Terry Thomson's will must be provided by a verified allegation, and notice must be given to each of them. See Probate Code section 17203(c)(1). 3) The address of Almarie Steward, alternate successor co-trustee named in the trust in the event the petitioner is unable or unwilling to serve, must be provided by a verified allegation, and notice must be given, absent a signed declination to serve. 4) The name and address of a proposed successor trustee of the Survivor's Trust must be provided by a verified allegation. The proposed trustee's acceptance of the trust and consent to act must also be filed, together with the beneficiaries' majority vote under section 7.1(b) of the trust, if any. 5) The name and address of a proposed successor trustee of the Bypass Trust must be provided by a verified allegation. The proposed trustee's acceptance of the trust and consent to act must also be filed, together with the beneficiaries' majority vote under section 7.1(b) of the trust, if any. 6) Please provide a description and fair market value of each asset on hand of the Survivor's Trust, plus estimated annual gross income therefrom, absent a bond waiver signed by each beneficiary and required by section 15602(b). 7) Please provide a description and fair market value of each asset on hand of the Bypass Trust, plus estimated annual gross income therefrom, absent a bond waiver signed by each beneficiary and required by section 15602(b). 8) Please provide a copy of the settlement agreement reached in 2023 and referred to in paragraph 9 of the petition. Note: An appearance may be required on 11/18/2024 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, unless papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CONNIE MARY MARATAS TRUST 1997; AND THE CONNIE M. MARATAS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/25/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) The fourth amended accounting petition was e-filed on 8/29/24, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. (2) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (3) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it a printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The notice of the irrevocability of a trust is not just a notice of death of the trustor. [Probate Code Section 16061.7.] (5) There were disbursements made to the conservator in 2022 shown in this fourth amended accounting, which cannot be confirmed yet, as the accounting in the related conservatorship proceeding has not yet been filed to include the period after 3/31/21. (6) The bank statements filed on 3/14/22 verified the ending balances as of 3/31/21, not 12/31/22. (7) The successor trustee verified the pleading on 8/24/23? Deadline is 11/15/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Given the death of the conservatee/beneficiary, the successor trustee might wish to see if the remainder beneficiaries will waive an accounting and agree to all of the relief requested.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TIMOTHY ANDREW TALBOT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1. No petition to remove current executor has been filed (which must be separate from the objection to the petition to terminate proceeding). 2. Other procedural issues: i. Petitioner requests waiver of bond, but Decedent's will only waives bond for those nominated under the will. ii. Petitioner alleges that the nominated successor executor (Young Bae Kim) should be precluded from serving due to conflict of interest, as he is Young Mi Kim's brother. Please elaborate. Attempts to obtain a declination from Young Bae Kim to serve as executor should also be alleged. iii. DE-147 has not been filed. iv. DE-147S has not been filed. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TIMOTHY ANDREW TALBOT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: Executor requests termination of proceedings; however, Probate Code 12251 only applies when there is no property belonging to the estate and subject to administration. Here, Executor does not allege that there is no property, only that the liabilities exceed the assets. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES D. BANKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/20/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was just e-filed on 9/16/24, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (2) No proof of service of the AMENDED petition has been filed. (3) No proof of publication of the AMENDED petition has been filed. (4) Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (5) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Deadline is 11/8/24 for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies. Courtesy copies to be provided: (1) proof of service of notice of the 11/20/24 hearing of the amended petition; (2) proof of publication of notice of the amended petition; (3) a supplement, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, providing the information missing in item 5a and about the decedent's parents. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A portion of item 3g(1) of the amended petition was still completed, although no part of item 3g(1) should be completed when there is no will.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL A GRISHAVER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/25/24. (No earlier date available.) (1) If the will is lost, both mailed and published notice must clearly indicate that the petition is to admit a LOST will. (2) If the petitioner seeks to admit a lost will, a copy of the will or the terms of the lost or destroyed will (a written statement of the testamentary words or their substance) must be submitted. [Probate Code Section 8223 and LRSF 14.20.] (3) Item 6 of the petition must be completed when a spouse survived but no issue. (4) Who was the decedent's mother and when did she die? [LRSF 14.18 C.] (Siblings are not heirs if a parent survived.) (5) Additional proofs of service have been filed without any indication why notice was given to those individuals. Item 8 of the petition was not supplemented to include additional interested parties. (6) Notice may not be mailed in care of another, unless the person is an adult and has directed in writing that notice be given to the other person. [CRC 7.51(a).] (7) If any of the additional parties to whom notice was mailed in care of another is a minor who is 12 years old or older, notice must be given directly to the minor and also to the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] (8) The petitioner might wish to petition for letters of special administration if he needs authority to look for a purported will. Deadline is 11/15/24 for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL A GRISHAVER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/25/24. (No earlier date available.) Aside from the proof of publication of notice, none of the other issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 9/16/24 have been addressed: no proposed order; no proof of service of notice; no Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number; no supplement to the petition completing the caption of her petition and providing the information missing in item 8 of the petition. No ex parte petition has been filed to dispense with notice to an interested party whose whereabouts are unknown. Deadline is 11/15/24 for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
